# Implementation Plan: Boss Office Phase Completion

**Branch**: `004-boss-office-completion` | **Date**: 2026-01-22 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/004-boss-office-completion/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Complete the Boss Office admin panel (Factory UI) to enable end-to-end tool management: Boss uploads source documents (.txt, .md, .pdf, .docx), Worker n8n workflow generates HTML tools following 3 principles, QA Department scores compliance, Boss reviews with responsive preview, Boss approves/rejects/requests revisions, and Deployment n8n workflow deploys to production URLs. This phase adds file upload with multi-format parsing, webhook receiver for QA-approved tools, responsive preview modal, complete review actions (approve/reject/revision), and Fast Track branding throughout the UI.

**Technical Approach**: Extend existing React + TypeScript Boss Office app with multer file upload backend, pdf-parse and mammoth parsers, webhook receiver endpoint, preview modal with iframe sandboxing, approve/reject/revision API endpoints that call n8n webhooks, and Fast Track brutalist styling (black/white/yellow colors, Plaak/Riforma fonts). Maintains clean architecture with Domain → Application → Infrastructure → Presentation layers.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.3 (backend), TypeScript 5.3 + React 18 (frontend), Node.js 20 LTS
**Primary Dependencies**: Express.js (backend API), multer (file uploads), pdf-parse (PDF parsing), mammoth (DOCX parsing), React 18, React Query v5 (API state), Vite (build tool), Tailwind CSS v3 (styling), Clerk (authentication)
**Storage**: MongoDB Atlas (fast_track_tools database, tools collection)
**Testing**: Manual testing for MVP (automated tests post-MVP)
**Target Platform**: Web application (backend: Node.js server, frontend: modern browsers - Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
**Project Type**: Web application (backend + frontend)
**Performance Goals**: File upload <5s (excluding n8n workflow), Preview load <2s, Approve action <3s (excluding webhook), Webhook receiver <500ms response time
**Constraints**: File upload max 10MB, n8n Worker webhook timeout 60s, React Query polling 7s interval, PDF parsing no OCR (text-based PDFs only), DOCX complex formatting may be lost
**Scale/Scope**: Single Boss user, estimated 37 tools (one per Fast Track sprint), 10-50 tools in review queue, <100 upload requests per day

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

### Constitutional Alignment

**✅ Domain-Driven Design** (Principle I):
- Tool entity remains aggregate root with tool_id
- Upload feature adds UploadRequest value object
- Webhook receiver stores tools with PrincipleCompliance value object
- Uses ubiquitous language: Boss, Factory, Worker, QA Department, Tool

**✅ Clean Architecture** (Principle II):
- Domain layer: Tool, UploadRequest entities (pure TypeScript, no dependencies)
- Application layer: UploadToolFromDocument, ReceiveQAApprovedTool, ApproveTool, RejectTool, RequestRevision use cases
- Infrastructure layer: multer file storage, pdf-parse/mammoth parsers, n8n webhook clients, MongoDB repository
- Presentation layer: Upload page, Preview modal, Review actions UI (calls use cases only)
- Dependencies point inward (domain knows nothing about multer, n8n, MongoDB)

**✅ Lean Startup with Human-in-the-Loop** (Principle III):
- Boss uploads document → Worker generates → QA scores → Boss reviews → Boss decides
- Measure: Boss approval rate on first generation, review time per tool
- Learn: Does file upload simplify Boss workflow vs manual tool creation?

**✅ Jobs To Be Done** (Principle IV):
- Boss job: "Help me turn my sprint documents into tools in minutes" → Upload feature delivers
- Boss job: "Help me review and approve tools quickly" → Preview + review actions deliver
- Boss job: "Help me ensure every tool meets Fast Track quality standards" → QA webhook receiver delivers

**✅ Spec-Driven Development** (Principle V):
- Spec.md created first with user stories, acceptance scenarios, functional requirements
- Plan.md (this document) generated from spec
- Tasks.md will follow from plan

**✅ Three-Principle Compliance** (Principle VI):
- Webhook receiver stores qa_score (brand/criteria/historical compliance scores)
- Boss sees compliance scores in review interface before approving
- Tools with <8/10 scores require Boss override with rationale

**✅ Single-File Tool Architecture** (Principle VII):
- Generated tools received via webhook are standalone HTML (no changes to this principle)
- Upload feature receives source documents, Worker generates single-file tools

**✅ Fast Track Brand Guidelines** (Compliance Check):
- All UI components will use #000000 (black), #FFFFFF (white), #FFF469 (yellow)
- Plaak Ex Condensed Bold for headings, Riforma LL Regular for body text
- Bold, minimalist, brutalist design with sharp corners
- Strong left alignment, generous whitespace
- Yellow used sparingly as accent (not decoration)

**✅ Ubiquitous Language**:
- Factory (Admin Panel), Boss (user), Worker (n8n generator), QA Department (compliance checker)
- Tool (generated HTML), Source Document (uploaded file), Principle Compliance (QA scores)
- All code and UI use constitutional terminology

**✅ Data Integrity & Privacy** (Principle IX):
- MongoDB tools collection uses tool_id unique index
- Clerk JWT authentication with Boss email whitelist
- No PII in generated tools (client_id passed via URL parameter)

**✅ Worker Observability** (Principle X):
- Webhook receiver logs all incoming requests to worker_logs collection
- Upload endpoint logs file processing duration
- Approve/reject/revision actions log n8n webhook calls

### Gate Status: ✅ PASS

All constitutional principles aligned. No violations requiring justification.


**Structure Decision**: Web application with separate backend (Express.js API) and frontend (React SPA). This structure follows constitutional clean architecture with clear layer boundaries. Backend uses domain-driven design with entities (Tool, UploadRequest), use cases (UploadToolFromDocument, ReceiveQAApprovedTool, ApproveTool), and infrastructure adapters (parsers, webhooks, MongoDB). Frontend uses presentation layer (React components) that calls application ports (HTTP client). This separation enables independent deployment, testing, and scaling of backend vs frontend.

## Complexity Tracking

> **Fill ONLY if Constitution Check has violations that must be justified**

No constitutional violations detected. All complexity is justified by requirements:

| Justification | Rationale |
|---------------|-----------|
| Four file parsers (txt, md, pdf, docx) | Constitutional requirement: Boss uploads various source document formats. Each format requires specific parsing library (pdf-parse, mammoth). Cannot use single parser. |
| Three n8n webhook clients (Worker, Deployment, Revision) | Constitutional requirement: Lean Startup with Human-in-the-Loop. Boss triggers Worker (generation), Deployment (approve), and Revision (changes) as separate workflows. Each workflow has distinct purpose. |
| Preview modal with three responsive modes | Constitutional requirement: Boss job "Help me review tools quickly". Must verify tool works across devices before deploying to clients. Cannot test responsiveness without preview modes. |
| Separate webhook receiver endpoint | Constitutional requirement: Worker Observability. QA-approved tools arrive via webhook asynchronously. Cannot block Boss's upload request waiting for Worker completion (60s timeout). Must receive tools independently. |

## Phase 0: Research & Unknowns

### Research Tasks

**R001: File Parser Libraries**
- **Question**: Which libraries provide reliable text extraction for PDF and DOCX files in Node.js?
- **Research**: Evaluate pdf-parse (PDF), pdfjs-dist (PDF), mammoth (DOCX), docx (DOCX)
- **Decision Criteria**: Accuracy (95%+ text extraction), npm popularity, TypeScript support, maintenance status
- **Output**: Selected libraries with installation commands and usage examples

**R002: Multer Configuration for Express**
- **Question**: How to configure multer for file uploads with 10MB limit, temporary storage, and cleanup?
- **Research**: Multer documentation, best practices for temporary file handling, security considerations
- **Decision Criteria**: Memory efficiency, security (prevent directory traversal), cleanup automation
- **Output**: Multer middleware configuration with file size limits and storage options

**R003: Iframe Sandboxing Best Practices**
- **Question**: Which sandbox attributes prevent XSS while allowing generated tool functionality?
- **Research**: MDN sandbox documentation, security implications of allow-scripts, allow-same-origin
- **Decision Criteria**: Security (prevent XSS), functionality (allow tool JavaScript to run)
- **Output**: Recommended sandbox attribute combination with security rationale

**R004: React Query Mutation Patterns**
- **Question**: How to structure mutations for upload, approve, reject, revision with optimistic updates and error handling?
- **Research**: React Query v5 mutation docs, error handling patterns, loading states
- **Decision Criteria**: UX (immediate feedback), reliability (error recovery)
- **Output**: Mutation hook patterns with onSuccess, onError, onMutate callbacks

**R005: Fast Track Font Loading**
- **Question**: How to load custom fonts (Plaak Ex Condensed Bold, Riforma LL Regular) in Tailwind CSS?
- **Research**: Tailwind font-family configuration, @font-face CSS, web font loading best practices
- **Decision Criteria**: Performance (minimize CLS), licensing (Fast Track owns fonts)
- **Output**: Tailwind config with font-family declarations and @font-face CSS

**R006: n8n Webhook Response Handling**
- **Question**: What response structure do n8n webhooks return (deployed_url, github_commit_sha)?
- **Research**: Review existing n8n workflow docs (Worker, QA, Deployment)
- **Decision Criteria**: Consistency with existing workflows
- **Output**: TypeScript interfaces for webhook response payloads

**R007: MongoDB Webhook Idempotency**
- **Question**: How to prevent duplicate tool creation if webhook is called multiple times?
- **Research**: MongoDB unique indexes, upsert patterns, idempotency keys
- **Decision Criteria**: Data integrity (no duplicates), performance (fast writes)
- **Output**: MongoDB schema with unique tool_id index and upsert strategy

## Phase 1: Design & Contracts

### Data Model (data-model.md)

**Entities**:

**Tool** (Aggregate Root - existing, extended):
```typescript
{
  tool_id: string                    // Unique identifier (e.g., "tool_001")
  source_document: SourceDocument    // NEW: File metadata
  tool_purpose: string               // What the tool does
  client_name: string                // Who it's for
  document_type: 'txt' | 'md' | 'pdf' | 'docx'  // NEW: File type
  generated_html: string             // Generated tool HTML
  generated_timestamp: Date          // When AI created it
  status: ToolStatus                 // pending_review | approved | rejected | deployed | revision_requested
  qa_score: number                   // 0-100 compliance score
  qa_feedback: string                // QA department comments
  qa_timestamp: Date                 // When QA scored it
  boss_review_status: 'approved' | 'rejected' | null
  boss_feedback: string | null       // Boss's comments
  boss_reviewed_at: Date | null      // When Boss reviewed
  deployed_url: string | null        // Live tool URL
  deployed_at: Date | null           // Deployment timestamp
  github_commit_sha: string | null   // Git commit hash
  revision_history: Revision[]       // Array of previous versions
}
```

**UploadRequest** (NEW - Value Object):
```typescript
{
  request_id: string                 // Unique upload ID (nanoid)
  file_name: string                  // Original filename
  file_size: number                  // Bytes
  file_type: 'txt' | 'md' | 'pdf' | 'docx'
  file_path: string                  // Temporary path on server
  tool_purpose: string               // Provided by Boss
  client_name: string                // Provided by Boss
  extracted_text: string             // Parsed content
  upload_timestamp: Date             // When uploaded
  worker_webhook_called_at: Date | null  // When Worker was triggered
  status: 'uploading' | 'processing' | 'completed' | 'failed'
}
```

**SourceDocument** (NEW - Value Object):
```typescript
{
  file_path: string                  // Path to uploaded file
  file_type: 'txt' | 'md' | 'pdf' | 'docx'
  file_size: number                  // Bytes
  uploaded_at: Date                  // Upload timestamp
  extracted_text: string             // Parsed content
}
```

**WebhookPayload** (NEW - Value Object):
```typescript
{
  webhook_id: string                 // Unique webhook call ID
  webhook_type: 'tool-ready' | 'deployment-result' | 'revision-result'
  received_at: Date                  // Timestamp
  payload_data: Record<string, any>  // JSON payload
  processing_status: 'pending' | 'processed' | 'failed'
}
```

**Relationships**:
- Tool has one SourceDocument (composition)
- Tool has many Revisions (one-to-many)
- UploadRequest results in one Tool (after Worker/QA completion)
- WebhookPayload creates or updates one Tool

**State Transitions**:
```
UploadRequest: uploading → processing → completed | failed
Tool: pending_review → approved → deployed
Tool: pending_review → rejected (terminal state)
Tool: pending_review → revision_requested → pending_review (cycle)
```

### API Contracts (contracts/)

**POST /api/boss/upload** (upload.yaml):
```yaml
openapi: 3.0.0
paths:
  /api/boss/upload:
    post:
      summary: Upload source document and trigger Worker workflow
      security:
        - ClerkJWT: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          multipart/form-data:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - file
                - tool_purpose
                - client_name
              properties:
                file:
                  type: string
                  format: binary
                  description: Source document (.txt, .md, .pdf, .docx)
                tool_purpose:
                  type: string
                  example: "Budget Calculator"
                client_name:
                  type: string
                  example: "Acme Corp"
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Upload successful, Worker triggered
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  request_id:
                    type: string
                  file_name:
                    type: string
                  file_size:
                    type: number
                  extracted_text:
                    type: string
                  worker_webhook_called_at:
                    type: string
                    format: date-time
        '400':
          description: Invalid file type or size >10MB
        '401':
          description: Unauthorized (not Boss)
        '500':
          description: Server error (file parsing failed)
```

**POST /api/boss/webhooks/tool-ready** (webhook.yaml):
```yaml
openapi: 3.0.0
paths:
  /api/boss/webhooks/tool-ready:
    post:
      summary: Receive QA-approved tool from n8n Boss Office Storage workflow
      security:
        - N8nWebhookSecret: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - tool_id
                - generated_html
                - qa_score
              properties:
                tool_id:
                  type: string
                tool_purpose:
                  type: string
                client_name:
                  type: string
                document_type:
                  type: string
                  enum: [txt, md, pdf, docx]
                source_document:
                  type: string
                generated_html:
                  type: string
                qa_score:
                  type: number
                  minimum: 0
                  maximum: 100
                qa_feedback:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Tool stored successfully
        '400':
          description: Invalid payload (missing required fields)
        '409':
          description: Conflict (tool_id already exists)
        '500':
          description: Server error
```

**PUT /api/boss/tools/:tool_id/approve** (approve.yaml):
```yaml
openapi: 3.0.0
paths:
  /api/boss/tools/{tool_id}/approve:
    put:
      summary: Approve tool and trigger deployment
      security:
        - ClerkJWT: []
      parameters:
        - name: tool_id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Tool approved and deployed
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  tool_id:
                    type: string
                  status:
                    type: string
                    enum: [deployed]
                  deployed_url:
                    type: string
                  github_commit_sha:
                    type: string
                  deployed_at:
                    type: string
                    format: date-time
        '404':
          description: Tool not found
        '409':
          description: Conflict (already deployed)
        '500':
          description: Deployment webhook failed
```

**PUT /api/boss/tools/:tool_id/reject** (reject.yaml):
```yaml
openapi: 3.0.0
paths:
  /api/boss/tools/{tool_id}/reject:
    put:
      summary: Reject tool with feedback
      security:
        - ClerkJWT: []
      parameters:
        - name: tool_id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - boss_feedback
              properties:
                boss_feedback:
                  type: string
                  example: "Does not match brand guidelines"
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Tool rejected
        '404':
          description: Tool not found
        '500':
          description: Server error
```

**PUT /api/boss/tools/:tool_id/request-revision** (revision.yaml):
```yaml
openapi: 3.0.0
paths:
  /api/boss/tools/{tool_id}/request-revision:
    put:
      summary: Request tool revision with change instructions
      security:
        - ClerkJWT: []
      parameters:
        - name: tool_id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - boss_feedback
              properties:
                boss_feedback:
                  type: string
                  example: "Make buttons larger and add yellow background"
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Revision requested, webhook called
        '404':
          description: Tool not found
        '500':
          description: Revision webhook failed
```

### Quickstart (quickstart.md)

Developer setup instructions for implementing this feature:

**Prerequisites**:
- Node.js 20 LTS installed
- MongoDB Atlas connection string (MONGODB_URI)
- Clerk authentication configured
- n8n Worker webhook URL (N8N_WORKER_WEBHOOK_URL)
- n8n Deployment webhook URL (N8N_DEPLOY_WEBHOOK_URL)

**Backend Setup**:
```bash
cd backend
npm install multer pdf-parse mammoth @types/multer @types/pdf-parse
mkdir -p uploads  # Temporary file storage
```

**Frontend Setup**:
```bash
cd boss-office-app
npm install react-dropzone  # For file upload UI
```

**Environment Variables** (.env):
```
# Existing
MONGODB_URI=mongodb+srv://...
CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_...
BOSS_EMAIL_WHITELIST=boss@fasttrack.com,ivan.h@fasttrack-europe.com

# NEW
N8N_WORKER_WEBHOOK_URL=https://n8n.fasttrack.com/webhook/worker-generate-tool
N8N_DEPLOY_WEBHOOK_URL=https://n8n.fasttrack.com/webhook/deploy-tool
N8N_REVISION_WEBHOOK_URL=https://n8n.fasttrack.com/webhook/request-revision
WEBHOOK_SECRET=<shared secret for validating n8n webhooks>
```

**MongoDB Indexes** (create-indexes.js):
```javascript
db.tools.createIndex({ "tool_id": 1 }, { unique: true });
db.tools.createIndex({ "status": 1, "generated_timestamp": -1 });
```

**Run Development Servers**:
```bash
# Terminal 1 - Backend
cd backend && npm run dev

# Terminal 2 - Frontend
cd boss-office-app && npm run dev
```

**Test Upload Flow**:
1. Navigate to http://localhost:5173/upload
2. Drag a .txt file into dropzone
3. Enter tool_purpose and client_name
4. Click Upload
5. Verify Worker webhook is called (check n8n logs)
6. Wait 30-60s for QA workflow
7. Verify tool appears in dashboard with status "pending_review"

### Agent Context Update

After completing Phase 1 design, run:

```bash
.specify/scripts/powershell/update-agent-context.ps1 -AgentType claude
```

This updates `.claude/project-context.md` with:
- New technologies: multer, pdf-parse, mammoth, react-dropzone
- New API endpoints: /api/boss/upload, /api/boss/webhooks/tool-ready
- New use cases: UploadToolFromDocument, ReceiveQAApprovedTool, ApproveTool, RejectTool, RequestRevision
- Fast Track branding requirements (#000000, #FFFFFF, #FFF469, Plaak, Riforma)

## Phase 2: Task Breakdown

**Phase 2 is executed by `/speckit.tasks` command, NOT by `/speckit.plan`.**

The tasks.md file will break down implementation into 50-100 granular tasks organized by:

**Backend Tasks** (~40 tasks):
- File upload endpoint with multer middleware
- File parsers (txt, md, pdf, docx)
- Worker webhook client
- Webhook receiver endpoint
- Approve/reject/revision endpoints
- Deployment/revision webhook clients
- Error handling and validation

**Frontend Tasks** (~35 tasks):
- Upload page with drag-and-drop
- File upload mutation and progress UI
- Preview modal with responsive modes
- Review actions panel (approve/reject/revision)
- Reject and revision modals
- Fast Track styling for all components
- React Query mutations

**Testing Tasks** (~10 tasks):
- Manual test file upload (.txt, .md, .pdf, .docx)
- Manual test webhook receiver with cURL
- Manual test approve/reject/revision actions
- Manual test preview responsive modes
- Visual design review (Fast Track branding)

**Integration Tasks** (~5 tasks):
- Environment variables configuration
- MongoDB indexes creation
- n8n webhook URL configuration
- Clerk authentication testing
- End-to-end upload → review → approve → deploy flow

## Implementation Priorities

**Phase 2A (CRITICAL - MVP Completion)**:
1. File upload backend with multer and parsers (FR-001 to FR-004)
2. Upload UI with drag-and-drop (FR-018, FR-019)
3. Worker webhook integration (FR-004)
4. Webhook receiver endpoint (FR-005 to FR-008)
5. Approve endpoint with deployment webhook (FR-012 to FR-014)

**Phase 2B (HIGH - Review Workflow Completion)**:
6. Preview modal with responsive modes (FR-009 to FR-011)
7. Reject endpoint with feedback (FR-015)
8. Revision endpoint with webhook (FR-016)
9. Review actions UI (FR-022)

**Phase 2C (MEDIUM - Polish & Branding)**:
10. Fast Track styling for all components (FR-023)
11. Toast notifications (FR-024)
12. Error handling and retry (FR-025)
13. Dashboard polling optimization (FR-020)
14. Tool detail metadata display (FR-021)

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Impact | Probability | Mitigation |
|------|--------|-------------|------------|
| **PDF parsing fails for scanned PDFs** | Boss cannot upload image-based PDFs | Medium | Document limitation in upload UI: "Text-based PDFs only. Scanned PDFs not supported." Boss uses OCR tool before uploading. |
| **DOCX complex formatting lost** | Generated tool doesn't match original formatting | Medium | Mammoth extracts plain text + basic formatting. Acceptable per constitutional "Single-File Tool Architecture" - tools are regenerated by Worker, not direct conversions. |
| **Worker webhook timeout (60s)** | Upload appears to hang for large documents | Low | Show upload success immediately after parsing, not after Worker completion. Boss sees tool in dashboard when webhook receiver gets it (7s polling delay). |
| **File upload 10MB limit blocks Boss** | Some source documents exceed limit | Low | Document limit in upload UI. Boss compresses PDF or splits document. Most Fast Track sprint documents <5MB. |
| **n8n webhook failure** | Tool stuck in "pending_review" without deployed_url | Medium | Retry button in UI. Boss can manually re-trigger deployment. Log webhook errors to MongoDB worker_logs for debugging. |
| **Duplicate tool_id from webhook** | Webhook called twice, creates duplicate | Low | MongoDB unique index on tool_id prevents duplicates. Webhook returns 409 Conflict on duplicate, which n8n can ignore. |
| **Iframe sandbox breaks tool functionality** | Generated tool JavaScript doesn't run | Low | Test sandbox attributes (allow-scripts allow-same-origin) with existing generated tools. Adjust if needed while maintaining XSS protection. |
| **Fast Track fonts not loading** | UI fallback to system fonts | Low | Host fonts locally in public/ directory. Use font-display: swap for immediate fallback. Fonts are critical for brand compliance - test early. |

## Success Metrics

**Phase Completion Criteria**:
- [ ] Boss can upload .txt, .md, .pdf, .docx files via drag-and-drop
- [ ] File parsing extracts text with 95%+ accuracy (tested with 20 sample documents)
- [ ] Worker webhook receives tool_purpose, client_name, source_document
- [ ] Webhook receiver stores QA-approved tools in MongoDB with status "pending_review"
- [ ] Webhook receiver returns 400 Bad Request for invalid payloads
- [ ] Webhook receiver returns 409 Conflict for duplicate tool_id
- [ ] Boss can preview tool in Desktop/Tablet/Mobile modes
- [ ] Preview iframe sandbox prevents XSS (tested with malicious HTML)
- [ ] Boss can approve tool and receive deployed_url within 90 seconds
- [ ] Boss can reject tool with feedback, tool disappears from Pending Review filter
- [ ] Boss can request revision with change instructions
- [ ] All UI components use Fast Track branding (#000, #FFF, #FFF469, Plaak, Riforma)
- [ ] Upload completes with success message in under 5 seconds (excluding n8n workflow)
- [ ] Preview loads in under 2 seconds
- [ ] Approve/reject/revision actions show loading states and complete in under 3 seconds (excluding webhook)
- [ ] Tools appear in dashboard within 7 seconds of webhook call (polling interval)

**User Acceptance**:
- [ ] Boss can upload Know Thyself source document (sprint 1) and see generated tool after 60s
- [ ] Boss can preview tool, verify compliance scores, and approve for deployment
- [ ] Boss receives deployed_url and can open live tool in browser
- [ ] Boss can reject low-quality tool with feedback and it no longer appears in review queue
- [ ] Boss can request revision, see tool return to dashboard after Worker regenerates

1. **Review this plan** with Boss for approval
2. **Run `/speckit.tasks`** to generate detailed task breakdown (tasks.md)
3. **Run `/speckit.implement`** to begin implementation
4. **Test with real Fast Track sprint documents** (Know Thyself, Future Readiness, etc.)
5. **Deploy to production** after Boss approves first 3 generated tools
